data.title: Visit Campus Overview
data.markdown:
# Visiting CU Denver

CU Denver is located in **Downtown Denver** on the **Auraria Campus**, shared with Metropolitan State University of Denver and Community College of Denver.

## Tour basics
- Tours typically highlight **academic buildings, student services, study spaces, dining, and transportation**.
- Plan for **walking outdoors** between buildings.

## What to bring
- Comfortable shoes
- Water
- Your questions about majors, admissions, housing, and student life

## Quick help
- If you're lost on campus, ask at the **Student Commons Building** information desk or check campus maps.

data.title: Getting to CU Denver (Transportation & Directions)
data.markdown:
# Getting Here

## Address + area
CU Denver is on the **Auraria Campus** in downtown Denver.

## Public transit (RTD)
- Light Rail and bus routes stop near campus.
- Nearby hubs: **Auraria West Station** and downtown connections.

## Driving + rideshare
- Use campus parking locations for garages and lots.
- Rideshare drop-off is easiest near the **Student Commons Building**.

## Bikes
- Bike racks are available around campus buildings.
- Consider using Denver’s bike lanes approaching downtown.

## Accessibility
- Campus routes include paved pathways and accessible building entrances.

data.title: Parking & Visitor Options
data.markdown:
# Parking for Visitors

## Visitor parking
- Visitors typically use campus garages or visitor-designated areas.

## How to choose where to park
Pick based on where you’re going first:
- **Admissions / Tours**: park closest to the **Student Commons Building**.
- **Engineering**: park near engineering buildings.
- **Library visit**: park near the main library area.

## Payment + time
- Garages and lots may use pay stations or mobile pay.
- Leave extra time for parking during peak class hours.

## Common questions
### Can I park for free?
Visitor options vary. Always check posted signs or campus parking info.

### Where do I get a visitor pass?
Follow the instructions posted at the garage entrance or payment stations.

data.title: Campus Map & Landmarks
data.markdown:
# Campus Map

Use the campus map to locate:
- Academic buildings
- Student services
- Libraries
- Dining
- Parking garages
- Transit stops

## Major landmarks to know
- **Student Commons Building**: common meeting point, services, dining nearby
- **Library**: major study and research hub
- **Engineering buildings**: labs, maker spaces, and classrooms

## Tour routing tip
If you’re planning a self-guided tour, walk in this order:
1. Student Commons Building (start)
2. Major academic building(s) for your program
3. Library
4. Dining / student space
5. Parking/transit return

data.title: Undergraduate Admissions (Tour FAQs)
data.markdown:
# Undergraduate Admissions

## What visitors ask most
### What are the admissions requirements?
Admissions review typically considers academics and other application factors.

### When are deadlines?
Deadlines vary by term (Fall/Spring) and applicant type.

### Can I talk to an admissions counselor?
Yes—admissions can connect you with a counselor for your situation.

## Financial aid + scholarships
Many visitors ask about:
- FAFSA and aid steps
- Scholarships
- Tuition estimates

## Next steps (typical)
1. Explore programs
2. Check deadlines
3. Submit application
4. Track your application status

data.title: Programs & Colleges (High-level Tour Aid)
data.markdown:
# Programs and Colleges

CU Denver offers programs across multiple colleges.

## Common tour questions
### Do you have [Major X]?
Start by checking the program list and then ask your tour guide about:
- course focus
- internships and projects
- labs and student organizations

### How hard is it to switch majors?
Switching is often possible, but requirements vary by program.

### What is class size like?
It varies by course level and department.

## Recommended tour strategy
If a visitor has a specific major interest, include:
- the department’s main building
- labs/maker spaces (if applicable)
- advising/student support relevant to that college

data.title: Student Services Hub (Tour FAQs)
data.markdown:
# Student Services

## Services visitors care about
- Academic advising
- Tutoring and learning support
- Career support
- Disability resources and accommodations
- Counseling / wellbeing resources

## Tour-friendly explanation
CU Denver’s student support is designed to help with:
- course planning
- study skills
- internships and job prep
- personal wellbeing

## Typical questions
### Where do I go if I need help in a class?
Start with tutoring/learning support and your instructor’s office hours.

### What support exists for first-generation students?
Ask about student success and advising support programs.

data.title: Library (Study Spaces, Printing, Research Help)
data.markdown:
# Library

## What students use the library for
- Quiet and collaborative study spaces
- Research support and databases
- Printing and scanning

## Tour talking points
- The library is a core academic resource and a popular study destination.

## Common questions
### Can I reserve study rooms?
Many libraries support room reservations; check library services.

### Do you offer research help?
Yes—librarians and research guides can help with finding sources.

data.title: Dining & Campus Life (Tour FAQs)
data.markdown:
# Dining and Campus Life

## Dining
Visitors often want to know:
- where to eat on or near campus
- whether there are grab-and-go options

## Student life highlights
- Student organizations and events
- Social and professional clubs
- Places to hang out between classes

## Common questions
### Is there a meal plan?
Options can vary by campus services.

### Where do students hang out?
Common hubs include student commons spaces and nearby dining areas.

data.title: Wi-Fi, Email, and Tech Access
data.markdown:
# Technology Access

## Wi-Fi
- Students typically use a campus Wi-Fi network with university credentials.

## Email and accounts
- New students receive university credentials for email and systems access.

## Classroom tech
Many classrooms include presentation systems; students often use laptops.

## Common questions
### Can visitors get Wi-Fi?
Visitor Wi-Fi availability varies—ask at the student commons/help desk.

data.title: Campus Safety (Tour FAQs)
data.markdown:
# Campus Safety

## Safety resources
- Campus safety services support students, staff, and visitors.

## Getting around safely
- Walk with friends at night
- Use well-lit paths
- Save campus safety contact information

## Common questions
### Is downtown Denver safe for students?
Like any city campus, safety improves with awareness and using campus resources.

### What should I do in an emergency?
Use campus emergency guidance and contact emergency services.

data.title: Accessibility & Accommodations
data.markdown:
# Accessibility

## Disability resources
Students can request accommodations for:
- coursework
- testing
- classroom access

## On a tour
- Routes can be adjusted for accessibility.
- Buildings generally have accessible entrances.

## Common questions
### How do I request accommodations?
Students typically work with a disability resources office and provide documentation.

data.title: Housing (Set Expectations for Tour Questions)
data.markdown:
# Housing

## A key point for visitors
CU Denver is a downtown campus; housing options may include:
- campus-affiliated housing (if available)
- nearby apartments
- commuting options

## Common questions
### Do most students live on campus?
Many students commute; some choose nearby housing.

### How do I find roommates?
Students often use university resources or student groups.

data.title: Academic Calendar (Deadlines Tour Visitors Ask About)
data.markdown:
# Academic Calendar

## What it includes
- Start/end dates for semesters
- Add/drop deadlines
- Tuition/fee payment deadlines
- Breaks and holidays

## Common tour questions
### When does the semester start?
Check the academic calendar for the specific term.

### When is the last day to add/drop?
Add/drop deadlines vary by session and course format.

data.title: Career Services (Internships & Job Prep)
data.markdown:
# Career Services

## What students use it for
- Resume and interview help
- Internship/job search support
- Career fairs and employer events

## Tour talking points
- Many programs connect students with real-world experience.

## Common questions
### Do you help students get internships?
Career services and departments often provide support and connections.

data.title: Academic Advising (First-Year Tour Questions)
data.markdown:
# Academic Advising

## What advising helps with
- Choosing classes
- Understanding degree requirements
- Planning graduation timeline

## Common tour questions
### Will someone help me pick classes?
Yes—advising supports schedule planning and degree progress.

### Can I double major or minor?
Often possible depending on requirements and time-to-degree.
